summary: Bondy's conjecture—that every digraph with all nontrivial strong components contains a cyclic spanning subdigraph with cyclomatic number at most $\alpha(D)$—remains open in general. The cases $\alpha(D) \leq 3$ were already settled before the 2013 OPG posting (Camion for $\alpha=1$, Chen–Manalastas for $\alpha=2$, and Thomassé unpublished for $\alpha=3$), but no post-2013 literature advancing the conjecture beyond these small cases was found.

cyclic spanning subdigraph cyclomatic number alpha strong digraph conjecture proof

Bondy "cyclomatic" OR "ear decomposition" digraph spanning "alpha" conjecture 2014 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 arXiv
digraph "cyclic spanning" "ear" independence number alpha proof conjecture 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025 arXiv
notes: The OPG page itself was unreachable (ECONNREFUSED). The Bessy–Thomassé paper proving Gallai's conjecture (Combinatorica, 2007) is pre-posting. arXiv:2007.02834 (non-separating spanning trees for alpha=2) and arXiv:2504.01918 (long-eared digraphs) are post-2013 but address different questions. No verifiable post-2013 paper directly concerning Bondy's cyclomatic-number conjecture was found across four distinct search queries.
